item.title: What sabbaticals provide in the AI age
item.description:
When AI-assisted applicants become ‘regrettable hires.’ The case for giving workers extended time off. The emojis that define the current job market
— via Charter

item.title: POV: Should organisations hire for adaptability over expertise?
item.description:
The hiring conversation has shifted dramatically over the past few years. Once, technical expertise was the gold standard, proof that a candidate could step in and deliver from day one. Today, that assumption is being challenged. AI is transforming jobs, industries are evolving at breakneck speed and the skills employees possess today may become outdated… It landed in the last few hours.
— via HR Katha

item.title: Jeena & Company gets Swati Ranade as group CHRO
item.description:
After heading human resources at Clearship Group for over four years, Swati Ranade has joined Jeena & Company as group chief human resources officer (GCHRO). She will be tasked with bridging business and people strategy at the technology-driven supply chain solutions firm. An alumna of the Maharashtra Institute of Labour Studies, Ranade began her… It landed in the last few hours.
— via HR Katha

item.title: Algorithmic Intermediation and the International Transmission of U.S. Monetary Policy
item.description:
This paper examines how algorithmic and AI-driven fund management shapes the international transmission of U.S. monetary policy to emerging markets. It argues that the key source of instability is not algorithmic intermediation itself, but the similarity of models across funds. When algorithms rely on similar signals and make correlated errors, their trades…
— via arXiv

item.title: Student Evaluation of Repeated AI Feedback Across a Semester of Writing
item.description:
Generative AI is increasingly used for feedback in higher education, but evidence from repeated classroom use remains limited. This short paper analyses 2988 reflective essay-feedback-appraisal instances from 283 Estonian bachelor students across one semester. Students obtained and assessed feedback from a self-selected AI tool using a uniform prompt. The…
— via arXiv

item.title: When Not to Automate: A Formal Protocol for Human Preservation in AI-Optimized Organizations
item.description:
Standard automation ROI misses four categories of systemic risk -- tacit knowledge erosion, resilience reduction, regulatory exposure, and socio-institutional capital degradation -- that affect long-term organizational performance. PHP-AIO (Protocol for Human Preservation in AI-Optimized Organizations) is a five-gate sequential decision protocol with a…
— via arXiv

item.title: Women entrepreneurs are less likely to leverage AI—but more likely to benefit from it
item.description:
Men are more likely than women to leverage AI when launching or running a business, but female entrepreneurs who embrace the technology are more likely to see a productivity boost. That’s according to a new study by online HR services provider Gusto, which found that 64% of men leveraged AI to launch a business in 2025, compared to 56% of women . Men were…
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: If you do these 3 things, you have self-discipline, according to a psychology PhD
item.description:
How can you tell if you have self-discipline ? We all know people who work out at 5 a.m., eat only healthy food, and floss after every meal. And other people who sleep until the afternoon, chomp on untoasted Pop-Tarts, and then head out to have fun with their friends. If you measure your own self-discipline on this spectrum, you may be on the wrong track.…
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: The workplace isn’t designed for older women
item.description:
My kids were finally almost grown, and I was in my late 50s, when my mother started to fall. Not once. A series of falls, each one taking a little more of her, until the word the doctors used was acute. My husband and I drove out into the high desert and hiked, because that is what we do when we can no longer think and have to anyway. Somewhere on that…
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: Where psychological safety breaks down, and what HR can do
item.description:
Helen Sanderson begins her new content series on psychological safety by breaking it into five foundations, with practical ways HR can spot and strengthen each in its systems. The post Where psychological safety breaks down, and what HR can do appeared first on HRZone .
— via HRZone

item.title: Secondhand harassment supports hostile work environment claim, 5th Circuit holds
item.description:
The plaintiff also showed that the employer’s investigation into her claims was “insufficiently prompt and thorough,” according to the decision.
— via HR Dive

item.title: HR State of the Union: Understanding AI’s Impact on Your Business
item.description:
Date & Time: Thursday, July 23, 2026 at 2:00 pm ET In this webinar, Allie K. Miller explores what human-centered AI looks like in practice, sharing real-world examples and practical strategies for implementing AI in ways that are scalable, measurable, and focused on delivering meaningful business results. The post HR State of the Union: Understanding AI’s…
— via HR Executive

item.title: Atlassian’s new HR hire will decide how work splits between humans and agents
item.description:
Atlassian VP Alicia Lenart talks about the new HR role for planning human and AI agent capacity, and why the company rejects AI mandates. The post Atlassian’s new HR hire will decide how work splits between humans and agents appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: Google DeepMind exec: U.S. needs ‘urgent action’ on AI governance
item.description:
Thought leader and Google DeepMind exec Demis Hassabis says the United States should convene a standards body to advance best practices. The post Google DeepMind exec: U.S. needs ‘urgent action’ on AI governance appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: 3 learning habits backed by neuroscience that high performers use
item.description:
Fully 70% of U.S. workers feel unprepared for today’s workforce. That’s not a data point you encounter and move past. It sits with you, especially when you’ve spent a career thinking about how learning works. What separates high performers today isn’t natural talent. The differentiator is how people use the brain science of learning to develop that kind of… It landed in the last few hours.
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: Nespresso must face African American’s race discrimination claim, court rules
item.description:
Allegations by a former employee that she was denied promotions in favor of less qualified and less experienced White employees stated a cause of action under Title VII, the court said.
— via HR Dive

item.title: Are You Biased Toward Job Candidates Who Reply Quickly? ^ H0993B
item.description:
Are You Biased Toward Job Candidates Who Reply Quickly? ^ H0993B Harvard Business Review
— via HBR

item.title: How AI ‘workslop’ is undermining your organization’s performance
item.description:
Combatting the workslop paradox is not about stopping employee laziness and does not require technical solutions, writes a Gartner analyst.
— via HR Dive

item.title: Meta’s AI penalized protected leave in layoffs, according to lawsuit
item.description:
Twenty-six former Meta workers claim AI layoff tools penalized protected leave, in what may be the first suit over AI-driven job cuts. The post Meta’s AI penalized protected leave in layoffs, according to lawsuit appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: Great Leaders Know Which Emotions Their Feedback Will Trigger ^ H098YE
item.description:
Great Leaders Know Which Emotions Their Feedback Will Trigger ^ H098YE Harvard Business Review
— via HBR

item.title: Why Employers Cannot Ignore the Degree Apprenticeship Shift
item.description:
https://www.hrdconnect.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Red-Text-40.png New research from ACCA shows that degree apprenticeships are now the preferred route for many UK school leavers, with eight in ten planning to earn while they learn. As employers compete for the next generation of talent, the findings highlight the growing importance of creating…
— via HRD Connect

item.title: Affordability Is Not Just Inflation: It’s Time For A Historic Shift In Wages.
item.description:
Fuel, transportation, housing, and food costs go up, but wage growth is far behind. Is this episodic or is there something bigger going on? A NYT article explains that in San Francisco a combined salary of $370,000 is not enough to find a nice apartment. And this issue is national. For 2026 YTD gas prices ... Read more» The post Affordability Is Not Just…
— via Josh Bersin

item.title: Investigating first-language bias in LLM-based automated essay scoring: A cross-prompt evaluation of an open-weight AI-model on TOEFL essays
item.description:
This study examines the cross-prompt generalization and first-language (L1) scoring effects of a LoRA-adapted open-weight large language model (Gemma-3-27B-it) applied to automated essay scoring. Using the identical model and inference configuration reported in "AiAWE: An Open-Source LLM Automated Writing Evaluation System Using LoRA-Adapted…
— via arXiv

item.title: Skeleton: Visual Authoring of Non-visual Data Experiences
item.description:
When sighted practitioners author accessible data visualizations, they build navigation structures (the nodes, edges, and input bindings that govern how assistive technologies traverse an interface) entirely in code, with no visual representation. Without a representation to react to, practitioners cannot develop judgment about what makes navigation good or…
— via arXiv

item.title: Penny: Transition Network Analysis of Learner-Chatbot Interactions in Scaffolded EFL Writing
item.description:
Generative AI chatbots promise to transform English as a Foreign Language (EFL) writing by providing immediate, personalised feedback. However, their pedagogical value depends on how learners engage with them - a process often treated as a "black box." This study uses Transition Network Analysis to model the temporal dynamics of Japanese EFL learners using…
— via arXiv

item.title: AI may conceal growing ‘learning debt’ for fast-changing roles
item.description:
Close to 3 in 10 workers surveyed by TalentLMS said they’ve delivered work they couldn’t fully explain if asked how they did it.
— via HR Dive

item.title: Invisible Learning: Building Skills at the Pace of Work
item.description:
Date & Time: Tuesday, August 18, 2026 at 2:00 pm ET Part of AI Week, this session features Cornerstone’s Kelly Carmody and Mike Siano who will explore how to move beyond traditional training to build skills continuously, in the flow of work. The post Invisible Learning: Building Skills at the Pace of Work appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: How HR Outsourcing Helps Growing Businesses Stay Compliant and Productive
item.description:
Stuart Gentle Publisher at Onrec 17 Jul 2026 | How HR Outsourcing Helps Growing Businesses Stay Compliant and Productive As businesses expand, managing employees becomes increasingly complex. Recruitment, onboarding, payroll coordination, performance management, disciplinary procedures, and compliance with employment legislation all require significant time…
— via Onrec

item.title: Nike nearly ended an investigation into its DEI practices—until Trump took office
item.description:
When the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission opened an investigation into Nike’s diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) practices, it was unusual for a number of reasons. The federal agency’s mission has long been to enforce antidiscrimination laws, which involve pursuing claims brought by workers who had faced bias over their race or gender or other… It landed in the last few hours.
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: Women and men in the C-suite see 2 different workplaces
item.description:
Every 20 years since 1965, Harvard Business Review has published an article with the results of a survey asking men and women executives nearly identical questions about the perception of women in executive roles. In 1965, 2,000 executives were surveyed, of which a “substantially large” portion of men viewed women in management roles negatively, because… It landed in the last few hours.
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: Layoffs and AI hiring tools are driving class action risk, corporate counsel say
item.description:
Corporate counsel ranks workforce changes as a top-class action trigger, with employment and AI hiring risk rising fastest at the state level. The post Layoffs and AI hiring tools are driving class action risk, corporate counsel say appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: The cruel ‘loyalty tax’ blindsiding workers who stayed at their jobs for years
item.description:
The days of cradle-to-grave jobs are as long gone as fully employer-funded pensions, medical insurance , and annual company picnics for staff and their families. But while most employees have accepted a hefty dose of employment precarity as a fact of today’s labor market, new data shows many longer-tenured workers are blindsided—and suffer debilitating…
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: Employers may soon find it harder to retain workers
item.description:
Frustrations over compensation and optimism about other opportunities may lead employees to leave their current roles over the next six months, per a new report.
— via HR Dive

item.title: How the right contact center culture can drive measurable ROI
item.description:
When agents feel like they have a career, not just a job, attrition drops — and lower turnover is a clear source of ROI, Morgan & Morgan’s Angie Flury said.
— via HR Dive

item.title: Remote work isn’t always a ‘reasonable’ accommodation: 4 tips for employers
item.description:
As with all reasonable accommodations, determining whether remote work must be allowed depends heavily on the facts of the individual situation.
— via HR Dive

item.title: Recruiter Job Description: Duties, Skills, and a Free Sample To Help You Hire
item.description:
Hiring a recruiter is one of the most important talent decisions your organization can make. The person you choose will shape how quickly you find candidates, how well you assess them, and how consistently you fill open roles. Recruiting pressure remains high. Nearly 70% of HR professionals still struggle to fill roles, while skills gaps… The post Recruiter…
— via AIHR

item.title: Research Roundup: The Death of the Cover Letter, Boring Meetings, Wide Salary Ranges, and More ^ H0999K
item.description:
Research Roundup: The Death of the Cover Letter, Boring Meetings, Wide Salary Ranges, and More ^ H0999K Harvard Business Review
— via HBR

item.title: Built for People: How to Design Work and Workplaces to Unlock Potential
item.description:
AI is rewriting how work gets done, and the organizations pulling ahead are treating it as a people transformation, not a technology rollout. That puts HR, leadership, and the workplace itself at the center.
— via Charter

item.title: 3 span-of-control questions HR leaders need to ask
item.description:
HR leaders can help the business make better span-of-control decisions by asking a different set of questions around management layers and more. The post 3 span-of-control questions HR leaders need to ask appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: What Is an AI HR Assistant? 6 Use Cases & How To Implement One
item.description:
An AI HR assistant can answer HR questions, surface policies, support onboarding, and reduce repetitive admin work. Gartner predicts that by 2030, 60% of HR work tasks will run through an intelligent agent or large language model (LLM) interface. But that doesn’t make HR less important. In fact, it underscores the importance of HR’s judgment,… The post What…
— via AIHR

item.title: Does Multi-Agent Debate Improve AI Feedback on Research Papers?
item.description:
Probably not, at least for meta-analyses in economics. In a pre-registered, identity-masked, within-paper experiment, the authors of 44 meta-analyses ranked three AI reports on their own paper by usefulness for improving it: a single pass by a frontier model against two multi-agent debate tools we built and expected to win. All reports were held to a common…
— via arXiv

item.title: AI skill demand rises over 17-fold in tech jobs, six-fold in non-tech jobs since 2020
item.description:
AI skills are now a baseline workplace capability across India. Demand for AI skills in technology jobs has seen a significant increase. Non-technology roles also show a substantial rise in AI skill mentions. Professionals with AI expertise command higher salaries and experience faster growth. Workforce development is crucial for realizing AI's full… It landed in the last few hours.
— via ETHRWorld

item.title: Mid-career and feeling stuck? Here’s how to reclaim your value
item.description:
Skim the headlines and today’s career story belongs to two groups: anxious new grads at the bottom and embattled executives at the top. U.S. entry-level postings have fallen about 35% since early 2023, according to labor research firm Revelio Labs . Above them, the structure is thinning: in a 2025 Korn Ferry survey of 15,000 professionals, 41% said their… It landed in the last few hours.
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: Jensen Huang says the more AI is used, the more ‘we have to hire’
item.description:
Between shrinking entry-level gigs, mass layoffs , and high rates of long-term unemployment , AI has been at the center of workers’ job-loss fears. Some tech executives have warned that AI could lead to job displacement, but others preach the exact opposite. One staunch believer that AI will actually create more jobs is Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang . In a recent… It landed in the last few hours.
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: How to build an ownership culture that keeps top talent
item.description:
HR retention fails not when employees pursue a higher salary or a better schedule. They lose them to the desire for independence and control. The post How to build an ownership culture that keeps top talent appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: This week in 5 numbers: Employees spend nearly a day ‘botsitting’ AI each week
item.description:
Here’s a roundup of numbers from the last week — including how much more revenue companies that hired more workers saw. It landed in the last few hours.
— via HR Dive

item.title: Adding talent — not cutting it — leads to better revenue growth, study finds
item.description:
Companies that hired more employees were more likely to see sustained growth even amid AI implementation and attempts to do “more with less,” Orgvue said.
— via HR Dive

item.title: Leadership Summit 2026: AT&T CEO John Stankey on Developing Technology and Talent in the AI Era
item.description:
Leadership Summit 2026: AT&T CEO John Stankey on Developing Technology and Talent in the AI Era Harvard Business Review
— via HBR

item.title: Research Roundup: The Death of the Cover Letter, Boring Meetings, Wide Salary Ranges, and More
item.description:
Research Roundup: The Death of the Cover Letter, Boring Meetings, Wide Salary Ranges, and More Harvard Business Review
— via HBR

item.title: AI’s next big mission is rewiring your workplace
item.description:
Artificial intelligence is reshaping every business, but the hardest challenge is rewiring the company around it. Companies that bet on equipping good staff with extraordinary technology will have a golden opportunity, while those that treat this moment as a headcount problem will shrink to fit it
— via McKinsey

item.title: Catch, Throw, Repeat: Planning for Human-Robot Partner Juggling
item.description:
Dynamic object exchange between humans and robots remains a challenging problem due to uncertainty in perception, timing, and contact-rich interaction. Human-robot juggling represents a particularly demanding instance of this problem, requiring precise real-time coordination, predictive motion planning with feedback control, and robustness to variability in…
— via arXiv

item.title: Dendrite: A Real-Time Python Application for Online Brain-Computer Interface Research and Development
item.description:
Online brain-computer interface research requires software that can acquire multimodal physiological data, train and update decoders, run live inference, and preserve the full experimental provenance in a reproducible workflow. We present Dendrite, a real-time brain-computer interface application in Python that brings signal acquisition, decoder training,… And it ships with code, so you can try it rather than just read about it.
— via arXiv

item.title: 15 Best HR Training Programs for 2026
item.description:
Only 21% of HR professionals have the competencies they need to tackle current and future workforce challenges. HR training is an essential tool to equip you with new skills and knowledge in the different areas of HR. With so many options, choosing a course that’s right for you can sometimes be challenging. We’ve broken down… The post 15 Best HR Training…
— via AIHR

item.title: 15 Employee Retention Strategies for 2026 To Keep Your Best People
item.description:
Employee retention strategies are the deliberate practices, policies, and programs HR uses to keep workers engaged and reduce turnover. They matter more now than ever, because hiring remains hard: 69% of US employers report difficulty finding the talent they need. When replacing people is this costly and slow, protecting the employees you already have…
— via AIHR

item.title: Innocuous-Seeming Data, Latent Ideology: Ideological Generalisation in Finetuned LLMs
item.description:
Finetuning language models on small, curated datasets is standard practice for adapting them to specific policies or domains. We show that finetuning on narrow, factually-defensible, moderation-passing data can cause broad ideological shifts across unrelated domains, while preserving general capabilities. Training GPT-4.1 on right- or left-leaning economics…
— via arXiv

item.title: Wabtec’s Freight Equipment Business appoints Saurabh Choudhary as head -HR & IR
item.description:
Saurabh K. Choudhary has been appointed as head of human resources & industrial relations for Wabtec Corporation’s Freight Equipment Business. Choudhary began his professional journey as a business development specialist with Benett Coleman and Co. in 2002. Less than three years later, he moved to Bharat Sanchar Nigam as business development manager, in May… It landed in the last few hours.
— via HR Katha

item.title: In 2026, EEOC solidifies its lockstep with Trump’s anti-DEI agenda
item.description:
This year, the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission has been prolific in its agenda-setting and enforcement plans regarding diversity, equity and inclusion at work.
— via HR Dive

item.title: Meta’s AI-based layoffs allegedly targeted workers who had taken protected leave
item.description:
The company used “a constellation of internal artificial-intelligence systems” to determine who would be included in its 10% reduction in force, per a lawsuit. It landed in the last few hours.
— via HR Dive

item.title: Fair chance hiring better received by applicants when a moral case is made, study finds
item.description:
Applicants often assign negative stereotypes to policies meant to expand opportunities to people with criminal histories, according to research published in the Journal of Management.
— via HR Dive

item.title: Corporate America’s morale is in the gutter. The solution could be this
item.description:
Across companies in America, many employees are pondering a question about work: Does what I do here matter ? That question is at the center of today’s workplace morale challenge. Similarly, employee engagement has remained under pressure: Gallup’s 2026 State of the Global Workplace reports that global engagement fell to 20% in 2025, down from a 23% peak in…
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: Nearly 2 million Americans are dealing with long-term unemployment
item.description:
Employers added 57,000 jobs in June, and the unemployment rate dropped to 4.2%, from 4.3% in May, according to the Bureau of Labor Statistics report released earlier this month. But the BLS report indicated another trend impacting millions of Americans: long-term unemployment. The current dip in unemployment was mostly due to a decrease in the labor force… It landed in the last few hours.
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: Average CHRO pay is now $3.7M. Who are the top-paid?
item.description:
A new study highlights how organizations are tying HR executive compensation “heavily” to business performance. The post Average CHRO pay is now $3.7M. Who are the top-paid? appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: What generative AI can and cannot do for HR, according to a Cornell professor
item.description:
Cornell Professor Chris Collins discusses how generative AI's function in human resources has already arrived as data aggregation. The post What generative AI can and cannot do for HR, according to a Cornell professor appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: Multi-Agent AI For Talent Acquisition Arrives: Eightfold, Paradox, Maki, Radancy, And More
item.description:
Today Eightfold introduced its AI powered Candidate Agent , which fits together with its AI Interview agent. Not only is this an important announcement for Eightfold as it goes after the red-hot high volume hiring market, it’s emblematic of the whole new Multi-Agent AI solutions coming in the world of HR 2030 . (I explain ... Read more» The post Multi-Agent… It landed in the last few hours.
— via Josh Bersin

item.title: Are You Biased Toward Job Candidates Who Reply Quickly?
item.description:
Are You Biased Toward Job Candidates Who Reply Quickly? Harvard Business Review
— via HBR

item.title: What is HR Automation? Examples, Benefits & Best Practices
item.description:
Rapid advancements in Human Resources automation have enabled HR departments to work more efficiently and effectively. Freed from tedious tasks, HR staff can devote more time to people-centered initiatives that support organizational goal achievement. Core HR functions, like recruiting, onboarding, offboarding, timekeeping, payroll, and leave tracking, are…
— via AIHR

item.title: If union membership were to triple, workers could get a 14.5% raise
item.description:
In 2025, the number of American workers represented by a union climbed to 16.5 million, the highest share of unionized workers in 16 years. During an era of declining union membership, this is no small feat. The number of workers who long to be part of a union, however, is far higher —more than 50 million. There’s a reason so many workers are clamoring for… It landed in the last few hours.
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: AI-Augmented Human Resource Management? Insights from German companies
item.description:
This study examines the integration of AI into Human Resource Management in German companies. We ask if and how AI-based technologies are \enquote{augmenting} human resource management. Organisations employ generative AI or predictive analytics to transform traditional human resource functions, to streamline routine tasks and to reallocate resources toward…
— via arXiv

item.title: Early Adoption of Agentic Coding Tools by GitHub Projects
item.description:
Agentic coding tools are increasingly capable of generating and submitting pull requests (PRs) to software projects, introducing new forms of human-agent collaboration in software development. While prior studies have examined PR-level outcomes of agent-generated contributions, less is known about how agentic coding tools are adopted and managed at the…
— via arXiv

item.title: From storytelling to strategy: Employer branding enters its ROI era
item.description:
Employer branding moved beyond storytelling to become a strategic, ROI-driven function. Organisations increasingly focused on aligning purpose, employee experience, and brand messaging while measuring impact through hiring outcomes, engagement, and reputation metrics have marked a shift toward more data-driven talent strategies. It landed in the last few hours.
— via ETHRWorld

item.title: The great AI layoff is turning into the great AI rehire
item.description:
For the past two years, a certain kind of corporate announcement has become almost routine. The company cuts staff. The company cites AI . The company moves on. The formula was clean, the earnings calls were tidy, and the narrative was simple enough to fit in a headline. The only problem is that it wasn’t true, and the consequences are now showing up in… It landed in the last few hours.
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: Meta faces discrimination lawsuit over AI use in mass layoffs
item.description:
In May, Meta laid off 10% of its workforce, impacting nearly 8,000 employees. On Monday, a group of current and former staffers filed a lawsuit alleging the company used AI to target those with disabilities or on medical or family leave for layoffs. Instead of using the judgment of managers, the lawsuit claims Meta used a “constellation of internal… It landed in the last few hours.
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan has slashed 40% of jobs in some departments, thanks to AI
item.description:
JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon has repeatedly suggested that AI adoption would not drive widespread layoffs at the bank. But on Tuesday, he disclosed that JPMorgan had made significant cuts to a number of departments due to AI. Dimon shared the changes during the company’s latest earnings call, in response to a question about whether artificial intelligence… It landed in the last few hours.
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: California community college settles with professor who sued over DEI policy
item.description:
Bakersfield College agreed to not require a professor to use diversity, equity, inclusion and accessibility principles in his teaching or scholarship.
— via HR Dive

item.title: The biggest workforce shortage in U.S. history is being overshadowed by AI
item.description:
New reporting projects the largest labor shortage in US history. Why HR leaders should add talent scarcity to their AI-era planning. The post The biggest workforce shortage in U.S. history is being overshadowed by AI appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: Latest workforce development funds target specific industries — like nuclear energy and AI
item.description:
The U.S. Chamber of Commerce Foundation and the U.S. Department of Labor separately announced grants and funding designed to target critical occupations.
— via HR Dive

item.title: How physician CEOs hone the double-edged sword of clinical training
item.description:
The journey from physician to CEO has no prescribed path, but those who’ve reached the top reveal how they evolved the strengths and instincts forged in clinical training for enterprise leadership.
— via McKinsey

item.title: Building expertise in the age of AI: Who trains the next generation?
item.description:
As AI reshapes entry-level work, organizations should rethink how expertise is developed by integrating knowledge management, role design, learning, and coaching into a single system.
— via McKinsey

item.title: ﻿The Global Scaling Gap: Why Strategic Clarity Is Crucial in the Age of AI
item.description:
Michael Glenwood Gibbs/theispot.com Digital platforms and generative AI have lowered the barriers to accessing global talent, capital, and knowledge for companies everywhere while making it possible to reach customers across languages and cultures. Research my colleagues and I have conducted suggests that such tools make it easier for entrepreneurs to serve…
— via MIT Sloan

item.title: Shared Bidding Algorithms and Competition: Evidence from Electricity Markets
item.description:
Competing firms increasingly delegate pricing and bidding decisions to algorithms supplied by the same third-party providers. We study whether a shared algorithm leads competitors to internalise one another's profits, using data from the Australian National Electricity Market, where every battery's bids are observed at 5-minute frequency and can be linked… It landed in the last few hours.
— via arXiv

item.title: Sensing the properties of virtual objects without physical feedback
item.description:
People who have interacted with simulated worlds and simulated objects in extended reality (XR) often have a sense that they can 'feel' the objects being simulated despite them not being physical. Our sense of touch is essential for how we 'feel' the physical world, however, there is an open question as to what it means to 'feel' virtual objects when… It landed in the last few hours.
— via arXiv

item.title: Which company offers interns $8,600 per week?
item.description:
Competition for talent continues to heat up as companies face a skills mismatch driven by AI adoption.
— via HR Dive

item.title: Aïra: Rethinking AI Research Assistants for Interdisciplinary Science
item.description:
Scientific discovery increasingly depends on interdisciplinary teams whose members contribute distinct expertise, conceptual frameworks, vocabularies, assumptions, and standards of evidence. Today's AI research assistants are largely designed to support individual researchers through literature review, writing assistance, coding, and data analysis. While…
— via arXiv

item.title: HR Perspectives by Kundan Kumar: “Diversity creates real value only when it is underpinned by inclusion”
item.description:
Merck Life Science sits at the intersection of science, technology and commerce, supplying the tools, materials and services that enable drug discovery, bioprocessing and laboratory research across the global life sciences industry. In India, that means managing a workforce that spans researchers, manufacturing specialists, sales professionals and shared… It landed in the last few hours.
— via HR Katha

item.title: Week in review: HR’s uphill battle for employee trust
item.description:
We’re rounding up last week’s top stories, including steps recruiters can take to ensure sourcing efforts don’t get lost in a sea of scams.
— via HR Dive

item.title: White employee’s lawsuit over ‘equity mindset’ requirement survives at 7th Circuit
item.description:
The plaintiff claimed that she refused to adopt her employer’s views because she viewed them as racist and discriminatory against White people.
— via HR Dive

item.title: Leadership still hasn’t accepted this key truth about performance
item.description:
This month marks the 15th anniversary of the publication of my book Lead From The Heart . While years have passed since then, I’m very much aware that the book’s central premise is one many leaders and organizations have yet to fully embrace—or perhaps don’t yet believe. In 2011, I argued that the most effective way to improve workplace performance isn’t to…
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: World Youth Skills Day: Why AI Is Changing What Employers Value Most
item.description:
https://www.hrdconnect.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Red-Text-39.png As AI reshapes the workplace, employers are placing greater value on a combination of technical capability and human skills. Marking World Youth Skills Day, this analysis explores how hiring priorities are evolving, why AI literacy is becoming essential and what organisations can do to…
— via HRD Connect

item.title: Today’s workforce planning question: Where (not whom) should we hire?
item.description:
The real bottleneck isn't a lack of information; it's a lack of a credible starting point.
— via HR Dive

item.title: 6 benefits market shifts facing HR leaders
item.description:
What benefits market shifts should HR leaders look hard at as the second half of 2026 begins? Get ahead of the situation. The post 6 benefits market shifts facing HR leaders appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: Wharton researchers explain how cognitive surrender takes hold when employees lean on AI
item.description:
Wharton research identifies cognitive surrender, a mode of thinking that emerges when employees rely on AI for decisions. The post Wharton researchers explain how cognitive surrender takes hold when employees lean on AI appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: Great Leaders Know Which Emotions Their Feedback Will Trigger
item.description:
Great Leaders Know Which Emotions Their Feedback Will Trigger Harvard Business Review It landed in the last few hours.
— via HBR

item.title: HandPad: A Bimanual Hand Interface for Fluid Window Interactions in VR
item.description:
Virtual Reality (VR) offers potential for productivity work by creating expansive displays anywhere, yet current systems often rely on external input devices that limit the on-the-go use of mobile VR. We introduce HandPad, a suite of bare-hand interaction techniques that leverage the benefits of asymmetric bimanual coordination and self-haptic support.…
— via arXiv

item.title: Playful AI in Professional Email: A Field Experiment on Tone and Recipient Engagement
item.description:
Large language models (LLMs) are rapidly reshaping workplace communication, yet whether AI-assisted writing changes how recipients actually behave, and through what channel, remains unknown. Here, in a randomized crossover field experiment, 121 employees across six companies sent work emails under three conditions over three weeks: unaided writing, GPT-5…
— via arXiv

item.title: PaperRouter-Agent: A Content-Grounded LLM Agent for Personalized Hierarchical Paper Routing
item.description:
Researchers organize the papers they collect into personal folder hierarchies in reference managers, and route each new paper into the folder where it belongs. This task differs from standard hierarchical text classification. A user's folder hierarchy is not a fixed, shared taxonomy but a private and evolving folksonomy whose folder meanings may be topical,…
— via arXiv

item.title: 13 Best Online HR Courses To Take in 2026
item.description:
Whether you’re looking to enter the HR field and build your knowledge or are a seasoned HR professional wanting to get certified and grow in your career, online HR courses are a great place to start. Although only some organizations require employees to be certified or have completed a formal HR training course, it can… The post 13 Best Online HR Courses To…
— via AIHR

item.title: LinkedIn: AI is rewriting the first job—and every job after it
item.description:
LinkedIn’s Aneesh Raman explains how AI rewrites work at every level and what it signals for how employers hire, develop and structure talent. The post LinkedIn: AI is rewriting the first job—and every job after it appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: Orissa HC restores pension in leave dispute
item.description:
The Orissa High Court has ruled that prolonged absence from duty without sanctioned leave does not automatically amount to misconduct serious enough to justify compulsory retirement or loss of retirement benefits. The case involved railway employee Jaya Chandra Mishra, who had applied for leave but went ahead with it even though it was not approved. [...]… It landed in the last few hours.
— via HR Katha

item.title: These are the application keywords that will bring in top creative talent
item.description:
A recent Resume.org survey of 991 U.S. hiring managers found that creative skills have surpassed technical ones in perceived value, with 57% saying creative employees are harder to replace with AI than technical workers. The World Economic Forum’s “ Future of Jobs Report 2025 ” found creative thinking to be a major priority for employers, ranking it almost…
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: Performance Management Needs New Metrics in the AI Era ^ H098J5
item.description:
Performance Management Needs New Metrics in the AI Era ^ H098J5 Harvard Business Review
— via HBR

item.title: How ‘phone booth’ soccer taught World Cup greats to achieve more with less
item.description:
Tech workers’ bigger AI fear than job loss. Why constraints matter for everyone from Messi to middle managers.The abandoned IBM office campus that’s luring urban trespassers.
— via Charter

item.title: 5 career truths nobody tells you
item.description:
Below, Emily Durham shares five key insights from her new book, Clock In: No-BS Advice for Getting Ahead in Your Career (Without Losing Your Mind) . Emily, also known as Emily the Recruiter on social media, is a recruiter-turned career-coach with an audience of over 3 million people across Instagram, TikTok , YouTube, and her podcast. What’s the big idea?…
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: Founder fired after allegedly breaching firm’s own return-to-office policy
item.description:
A legal dispute at an investment firm is putting leadership accountability and workplace attendance policies under fresh scrutiny. It landed in the last few hours.
— via HRreview

item.title: Bhavin Vahia is now CHRO, Transformers & Rectifiers India
item.description:
After a successful tenure of almost a decade with the Zydus Group, Bhavin Vahia has moved to Transformers & Rectifiers India (TARIL) as chief human resources officer (CHRO). This alumnus of Maharaj Sayajirao University of Baroda and XLRI Jamshedpur began his professional journey as a humble HR executive with JMC Projects in 2002. Over a [...] The post… It landed in the last few hours.
— via HR Katha

item.title: AI Adoption in S&P 500 Firms
item.description:
The adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) by large enterprises is an important potential source of aggregate productivity improvement and labor market impact. We study AI adoption of S&P 500 firms over the period 2016 to 2025, estimating adoption at the enterprise level. While generative AI tools are useful for personal and professional applications, our…
— via arXiv

item.title: Regional Economic Impacts of the Just Energy Transition: Lessons for Coal Regions
item.description:
The coal phase-out's regional economic impact is a key challenge of the energy transition, as employment and fiscal dependence in coal regions face structural adjustment without automatic market solutions. Analyzing European Union NUTS 2 regions from 2000-2022 with fixed effects and clustered errors, coal regions show a consistent 1.1 percentage points…
— via arXiv

item.title: Epilepsy Online Social Support: Characterizing Topics and Challenges Shared in the r/Epilepsy Community
item.description:
Epilepsy is one of the most common neurological conditions, and people living with epilepsy (PLWE) often use social media as a resource. However, a comprehensive understanding of the topics represented in epilepsy-specific communities where PLWE may be more honest is essential to designing better technologies to address epilepsy self-management. To…
— via arXiv

item.title: Why you should document your processes as a solopreneur
item.description:
I’m a process fiend. But most of the time, the “how this works” knowledge exists only in my head. As a solo business owner , this was mostly fine… until I needed to collaborate with other people . Most solopreneurs treat process documentation as a someday task. There’s always something more pressing—like a client deadline, invoices to send, or a proposal to…
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: The benefit workers need amid sky-high inflation
item.description:
A more sustainable solution to help workers make ends meet—and avoid taking out costly loans or drawing down retirement accounts—is to offer emergency savings as a workplace benefit.
— via Charter

item.title: Surge pricing is broken – but there’s a smarter way to match gig workers with consumers
item.description:
Surge pricing is unpopular with consumers and drivers alike, but there are ways to reduce its inherent inefficiencies. charlesdeluvio on Unsplash Millions of Americans tap their smartphone screens daily to order rides , groceries or dinner , hooked on the seamless convenience of the gig economy. Yet beneath this frictionless interface lies a brewing war. On…
— via The Conversation Business

item.title: The economy is shutting young adults out of career-entry jobs, analysis finds
item.description:
Artificial intelligence matters, but in a “narrow, early and age-specific way,” researchers at the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis said.
— via HR Dive

item.title: Do you really have an HR strategy?
item.description:
HR should have a strategy, but how can it if the business itself doesn’t have a clear or consistent strategy? The post Do you really have an HR strategy? appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: Companies keep talking about the motherhood penalty. They’re missing the motherhood advantage
item.description:
Motherhood is one of the most intense leadership incubators many women will ever enter. And when mothers are supported, the growth can be profound. However, that is not the narrative most workplaces carry. I have worked with many clients who find that when they become mothers, the old rules of ambition do not quite fit in the same way. They still care… It landed in the last few hours.
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: In a tough job market, don’t make AI your only career coach
item.description:
If you’re like most people, you’re using artificial intelligence in a variety of ways, and you may be relying on its help as a partner in this tough job market. You’re probably even asking AI for career advice. But is this a good idea? The answer is decidedly mixed. For some aspects of career guidance, AI can be helpful. But there are some very big caution…
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: What 60 Years of Data Reveals About How Men and Women Experience Leadership ^ H098JS
item.description:
What 60 Years of Data Reveals About How Men and Women Experience Leadership ^ H098JS Harvard Business Review
— via HBR

item.title: Surge pricing hurts gig workers and consumers. Here’s how to fix it
item.description:
Millions of Americans tap their smartphone screens daily to order rides , groceries, or dinner , hooked on the seamless convenience of the gig economy. Yet beneath this frictionless interface lies a brewing war. On one side, consumers are squeezed by volatile and erratic surge pricing, which adjusts to reflect spiking demand or dropping supply. On the other…
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: AI fluency is your top hiring requirement. So, why are bad hires still happening?
item.description:
Ninety-five percent of U.S. organizations have made AI fluency a hiring requirement, yet 59% report bad hires anyway. The post AI fluency is your top hiring requirement. So, why are bad hires still happening? appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: Even in conservative North Dakota, some socialist institutions thrive
item.description:
The Bank of North Dakota is state-owned and unique in the United States. AP Photo/Dale Wetzel Candidates affiliated with Democratic Socialists of America, an organization well to the left of the Democratic Party , have scored wins in several of the party’s competitive primaries . It’s very likely that they will win House seats in the November 2026 elections…
— via The Conversation Business

item.title: Germany moves to require doctor’s notes on day one of sick leave
item.description:
The plan scraps pandemic-era phone certification and cites an average of 18 sick days per employee, drawing pushback from physicians. The post Germany moves to require doctor’s notes on day one of sick leave appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: The 5 skills recruiters are prioritizing over AI
item.description:
Human skills surged to the top of the list of capabilities recruiters are looking for today. But AI skills are also on the rise. The post The 5 skills recruiters are prioritizing over AI appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: Key Takeaways from Bain's 2026 Private Equity Midyear Report
item.description:
Key Takeaways from Bain's 2026 Private Equity Midyear Report Bain & Company
— via Bain

item.title: Make Talent Development Part of Your AI Strategy
item.description:
Make Talent Development Part of Your AI Strategy Harvard Business Review
— via HBR

item.title: The management role is broken. And we built it that way
item.description:
It’s not that managers are incapable. It’s that the role has lost coherence. Sarah Birtwistle draws on new research from Ipsos to explore the role design failure and plan a way forward. The post The management role is broken. And we built it that way appeared first on HRZone .
— via HRZone

item.title: Diagnosing and Repairing Persona Collapse in LLM Advice
item.description:
LLMs are increasingly used for personal advice on relationships, work, moral dilemmas, and crises. Post-training selects a stable, prosocial Assistant persona, but good advice requires more than a good default character: a skilled advisor comforts someone in crisis, challenges someone in denial, and stays procedural with a logistical question. We formalize…
— via arXiv

item.title: A Comparative Review of Methods to Create a Composite Index for Sustainable and Inclusive Wellbeing
item.description:
Societal goals need to shift from over-reliance on gross domestic product (GDP) to broader aspects of sustainable and inclusive wellbeing (SIW). However, defining SIW and eventually measuring it with a single number is problematic because it involves many subjective and objective contributors that combine in complex, non-linear ways. Conventional approaches…
— via arXiv

item.title: Onida names Neelam Sanguri as CPO
item.description:
Onida has appointed Neelam Sanguri as its chief people officer, strengthening its leadership team as the consumer electronics brand continues to focus on talent, culture, and organisational transformation. In her new role, Sanguri will lead Onida’s people strategy, with responsibility for talent management, leadership development, organisational culture,…
— via HR Katha

item.title: Tampa employee of Cuban origin wasn’t subjected to unlawful harassment, court rules
item.description:
The alleged incidents were sporadic, contained no racial or ethnic slurs, and for the most part, made no express reference to Hispanics. It landed in the last few hours.
— via HR Dive

item.title: Is our whole model of leadership wrong?
item.description:
My husband and I stumbled into Imago therapy early in our marriage. We were both open to therapy because we knew enough to know what we didn’t know. He came from a divorce, and I came from witnessing parents who were married for 48 years but continually had the same arguments over and over again. We didn’t want to repeat those mistakes. Imago therapy… It landed in the last few hours.
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: How to become a Goldilocks solopreneur: Not too many projects or too few
item.description:
The frustration I hear most often about self-employment is the instability. Moments when there’s too much work and you’re feeling stretched thin. And less ideally, the lulls when you’re between projects and your cash flow slows down or dries up. Frequently referred to as the “feast or famine cycle,” it’s the trap many solopreneurs experience when their…
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: Risks and Rewards of AI Leadership Development at Scale
item.description:
Date & Time: Tuesday, September 01, 2026 at 1:00 pm ET In this session, Alan Nelson of Admired Leadership and Brittany Podolak, Chief People Officer of athenahealth, discuss lessons learned from deploying AI tools to support leadership development and learning at scale inside athenahealth. The post Risks and Rewards of AI Leadership Development at Scale…
— via HR Executive

item.title: Top 10 priorities shaping the future of wealth management leadership
item.description:
Top 10 priorities shaping the future of wealth management leadership EY It landed in the last few hours.
— via EY

item.title: What 60 Years of Data Reveals About How Men and Women Experience Leadership
item.description:
What 60 Years of Data Reveals About How Men and Women Experience Leadership Harvard Business Review
— via HBR

item.title: Title VII plaintiffs don’t need to mitigate emotional distress damages, 5th Circuit says
item.description:
No such requirement exists in the law’s statute, the court held, rejecting an argument advanced by SkyWest Airlines in a former employee’s harassment lawsuit. It landed in the last few hours.
— via HR Dive

item.title: LinkedIn is the ‘most AI-saturated platform,’ new study suggests
item.description:
The amount of AI -generated content you scroll through might be far greater than you think, according to a new study by the AI detection platform Pangram. Pangram asked users of its Chrome extension to opt in to sharing their browsing results with the platform. From those results, Pangram used its AI detection model—which has a 0.01% false positive rate,… It landed in the last few hours.
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: Climate planning has prioritized floods. Heat demands equal attention
item.description:
Summer in the Northern Hemisphere has gotten off to a very hot start. This has focused attention on the impacts of heat on health, infrastructure, and economic productivity—and how to best manage them. That is a good thing. While past discourse on adaptation has often focused on the impacts of severe flooding, our recent McKinsey Global Institute report on…
— via McKinsey

item.title: AI skills now listed in 73% of tech job postings
item.description:
Highly regulated industries working to shape their AI implementation plans outpace other industries in the search for talent, according to Dice.
— via HR Dive

item.title: Human Resources Director: Role, Salary & How To Become One
item.description:
The Human Resources Director sits at the top of the HR function, shaping how an entire organization hires, develops, and supports its people. It’s one of the most senior roles in HR, and for many professionals, it’s the goal that guides a decade of career moves. If you’re aiming for that seat, you need a… The post Human Resources Director: Role, Salary &…
— via AIHR

item.title: Why better-off cities and towns see more benefits from data centers than rural regions
item.description:
After a rush to attract data centers with incentives, more communities are pushing back against their development. AP Photo/Ted Shaffrey The fierce backlash against data centers shows no sign of easing up. Since early 2024, more than 1,200 public actions – including zoning fights, public campaigns and temporary moratoriums – have been logged by the Data…
— via The Conversation Business

item.title: Feedback Manipulation Regularization: Enabling Offline Agent Alignment for Imitation Learning
item.description:
Reinforcement learning (RL) research has increasingly shifted focus towards alignment, ensuring agents learn behaviors adhering to human values. While human demonstrations and feedback have proven crucial for alignment, existing approaches predominantly combine these signals using multi-stage pipelines designed for the contextual bandit framing of language…
— via arXiv

item.title: TCS adds over 9,200 employees in Q1, records strongest hiring in nearly four years
item.description:
Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) added 9,279 employees on a net basis during the first quarter of FY27, marking its strongest quarterly hiring in nearly four years and signalling renewed workforce expansion despite a subdued demand environment. According to the company’s quarterly fact sheet, TCS’ total headcount stood at 593,798 as of 30 June, 2026. The… It landed in the last few hours.
— via HR Katha

item.title: DC Water to pay roughly $217,000 to settle claim it replaced HR worker with younger employee
item.description:
The District of Columbia Water and Sewer Authority must also enhance its nondiscrimination policies and provide advanced antidiscrimination training. It landed in the last few hours.
— via HR Dive

item.title: Half of GenZ Feel Guilty Using AI at Work Despite Growing Employer Demand
item.description:
https://www.hrdconnect.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Red-Text-38.png AI skills have become one of the UK's most sought-after hiring attributes, yet many younger employees still feel uncomfortable using the technology at work. New global research from Employment Hero reveals that half of Gen Z workers experience "AI guilt", with many using AI without their…
— via HRD Connect

item.title: On its 60th birthday, SHRM’s nonprofit arm pushes for better caregiver support
item.description:
Employers can’t reliably address working caregivers’ needs through policies and employee benefits alone, said Wendi Safstrom, president of the SHRM Foundation.
— via HR Dive

item.title: Meet the 2026 Top 100 HR Tech Influencers
item.description:
HR Executive's 2026 Top 100 HR Tech Influencers list honors the voices shaping workforce technology and the future of HR. The post Meet the 2026 Top 100 HR Tech Influencers appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: ‘Botsitting’ is wasting more than 6 employee hours a week
item.description:
Employees spend as much time checking an AI tool's work as they do producing outcomes, creating a new HR catchprase—and a productivity drain. The post ‘Botsitting’ is wasting more than 6 employee hours a week appeared first on HR Executive .
— via HR Executive

item.title: The Hidden Cost of AI-Assisted Creativity
item.description:
Chris Gash/theispot.com The Research The authors synthesized findings from four studies spanning short-story writing, circular-economy solutions, humor caption contests, and collaborative storytelling. Across all four studies, AI assistance improved individual output quality, but it reduced collective diversity, resulting in more similar, convergent ideas…
— via MIT Sloan

item.title: Leadership Summit 2026: Honest Company CEO Carla Vernón on Leading with Focus in Turbulent Times
item.description:
Leadership Summit 2026: Honest Company CEO Carla Vernón on Leading with Focus in Turbulent Times Harvard Business Review
— via HBR

item.title: VEGAS: Human-Aligned Video Caption Evaluation via Gaze
item.description:
Vision-language models excel at video captioning, yet typically generate descriptions that fail to capture individual viewers' attention. We propose VEGAS (Video caption Evaluation via GAze Score), a training-free metric that leverages test-time gaze to sample personalized, attention-aligned text. It is a cross-modal, information-theoretic metric that…
— via arXiv

item.title: Sharing economy in the era of full automation: Evidence from autonomous vehicle on-demand mobility services
item.description:
The digital age has facilitated the sharing of underutilized assets. This paper focuses on privately owned autonomous vehicles (AVs), a unique class of robots that can move independently and provide transportation services. When not in personal use, private AV owners can lease their vehicles to a platform that operates an on-demand mobility service (MoD).…
— via arXiv

item.title: From Thesis to Transition: An INSIGHT-Inspired Approach to Co-Designing Industry 5.0 Competency Pathways for Early-Stage Researchers
item.description:
Europe faces a critical "translation gap" where doctoral excellence in academia often fails to convert into industrial impact. While Industry 5.0 demands a blend of technical depth, sustainability, and human-centric design, traditional higher academic education remains siloed. This paper presents an approach from the Horizon Europe INSIGHT initiative to…
— via arXiv
